item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the addition of the Fifthists to the CotBG vs Sarkic dichotomy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noting that some very small edits were made.

@abstruse: Thank you for your comment and for pointing the typo, fixed!

Also made some very small layout changes to it.2 to see if it looks better on mobile devices, and added the tags "broken-god" and "sarkic" since SCP-2217 is explicitly mentioned in it.2 and the last iteration refers to Sarkicism motifs via SCP-4568-2. Tag staff: please inform me/revert if the tags were misapplied, thanks in advance.

@OriCat: Thank you for your support ^^ Also, @Sinon as well: I gave the article a title a couple weeks ago (although I might eventually change it)— was waiting a bit to see if this'd stay up, hence the delay. Thanks c:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it!

Errors: "mantain" should be "maintain" (x3). I can never remember how to access/edit fragments or I'd fix it myself… Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second Sinon's recommendation, and express my continued support.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suggest you give the article a title.

item.title: Welcome to my third SCP!
item.content:encoded:
Well, hello again! Welcome to my second third SCP article and overall contribution to the wiki. This actually is something I had prepared a while ago and was ready to post since at least a couple weeks ago, but then the 5000 contest happened so I decided to wait until the posting period was over so this didn't end in page 5 ten minutes after submitting the file to the wiki.

+ show block
– hide block

First of all, I would like to thank OriCat once again for their extremely thorough feedback and support during the preparation of this article. I am extremely thankful, and hopefully I gave justice to the underlying ideas behind this article. I'd also like to thank Reverend Fox for their offer to critique this article as well, even though unfortunately didn't come to pass (my fault entirely— I disappeared for 2-3 weeks from the drafts forum due to a trip). Regardless, I very much appreciate the intention to help.

This started as a very straightforward adaptation of the myth of Kaikai Vilu and Trentren Vilu, a deluge myth from Mapuche and Chilote mythology. (I must have that version in a .txt file somewhere…) With time, it became… something else. I am afraid I might have went too overboard in the other direction, but most of the motivations and characterization of the two snakes are based on ideas from the original mythology.

One of the things I like about this wiki is the massive, interconnected narrative universe that allows for a lot of interesting interactions. I won't deny that the original reason for this article's existence is that when the Hiemal object class was introduced the first thought that came to my mind was "if the SCPF had an article on these two snakes this would be a textbook example of Hiemal", but I never got to expand on that idea until now. When the idea came back to me, I decided to explore how the Foundation would have discovered the anomaly; this, in turn, led me to question how they would fit in the Foundationverse— and then: what would happen if relatively "minor-key" local mythological figures were to interact with the Foundation deities? Would they take sides? Would these sides reflect their original motives, as represented by the associated myth?

This SCP file is also very personal to me, for reasons detailed below. Since they have to do with politics to some degree, I'd rather enclose them in a collapsible for people who are not interested or are bothered by mentions of politics in author commentary. I need to mention, though, that these political stances aren't present in the text itself (at least, not intentionally) but they were a strong factor for me deciding to dig up this old idea and try to make it justice as best as I could. Feel free to skip this part if you so desire, as it is not directly relevant to the article. And also very long. I suck at brevity, it seems…


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn
Politics stuff, collapsed for people who may prefer to skip this part
Thanks for taking the time to read this

As you probably don't know since I am not very active in this wiki in general, I am from Chile, a country which is under severe political/social unrest at the moment. Due to reasons related to my studies, I was (and still am) abroad at the moment this started, but most of my family and friends are still there. This led to a strong feeling of uselessness/worthlessness, in a "I'm safe here, away from everything that's happening, taking the easy way out. What can I do to help? Nothing?" sense.

Of course, this is not really a good mindset, but as the news of police brutality and repression came this feeling became stronger. I mean, when you hear that 400 people have had some degree of eye injury and partial loss of vision because the police doesn't know (and doesn't care) how to use nonlethal weaponry properly, that on the early days of the unrest several hundred cases of sexual abuse and torture cropped up, it's hard to be optimistic— these were things that weren't done so blatantly since the 1973-1990 Pinochet dictatorship. Of course, the riots have been quite damaging as well (get a lot of angry people in an environment where they are sure nobody will hear them because nobody cares about their demands, and add serious incompetence from the government in handling the situation, and…) but that certainly does not justify things that can only be called state-sponsored human rights violation.

I was really, really scared on the first days of this, when things started. What would happen to my family, and my friends? What if they got caught in the middle of a riot, or if some overzealous policeman decided that they looked suspicious? Things have calmed down somewhat in the time since October, and some glimpses of potential improvement have appeared, such as a motion to change the current Constitution (which was one of the last "legacies" of Pinochet, designed to make the act of promoting and implementing most social reforms, e.g., healthcare or retirement, as hard as possible) and other important issues were addressed seriously for the first time in MEKHANE-knows-how-many years.

But the root of the issues still stands, and I don't expect the social unrest to fully end anytime soon. Thus, I still feel that I am doing nothing to help, to a degree. So, I've come to the conclusion that the best I can do is to raise awareness of Chile and of its current situation in any way I can— one of them being the creation of a SCP file both set in Chile and related to its local culture and mythology. This SCP, by design, couldn't have been set anywhere else. And so, writing this was also a coping strategy for me. I hope that this doesn't dampen any possible enjoyment you might've derived from it (I understand that politics, moreso those related to social issues, are extremely touchy subjects specially on the internet) but I wanted to give my rationale behind this article and my reasons for writing and posting it. Thanks for reading this.

If you are interested in knowing more about the ongoing situation, this Cracked article might be a good place to start, despite any personal opinions one may have about Cracked itself, heh. The Twitter account of the National Institute of Human Rights is also a good resource if you can read Spanish.

Forum staff: I understand if the text on the collapsible above goes against forum rules to any degree. If so, please inform me and I will delete it as soon as possible. Thanks!

On a side note, I know some people still dislike esoteric object classes. And I went and used not one, but two. I hope this doesn't make people too angry, whoops…

It is also worth noting that this SCP references some real life events. Hopefully I wasn't too tasteless in the incorporation of these elements…

Thanks for reading! Hopefully it was an enjoyable read. Any feedback would be appreciated, as usual!
